title: Lithocarpus inversus
taxonomy: Lithocarpus inversus is classified as a species of tree within the family Fagaceae. This family also includes other types of oak and beech trees. Lithocarpus inversus is a member of the genus Lithocarpus, which is native to Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands. The species is further classified as a member of the order Fagales and the class Magnoliopsida. It is a type of evergreen tree that is commonly found in tropical forests.

intro: Lithocarpus inversus, also known as the Chinese chinquapin or Taiwan chinquapin, is a species of plant in the beech family (Fagaceae) that is native to Taiwan and southern China. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat a variety of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus inversus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lithocarpus inversus is commonly used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and extracts, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
